mission: Identify cheapest delay-supported QPU to redo F-N12-1 Orch-OR test after Forte 1 INDETERMINATE

candidates.platform: IBM Quantum Cloud (direct, NOT via AWS/Azure)
candidates.hardware_open_plan: Heron r2 (ibm_kingston) — 156 qubits, 340k CLOPS, median 2Q error 2.03e-3
candidates.hardware_paid: Nighthawk (median T1 350 us), Eagle 127q, additional Heron r1/r2
candidates.delay_primitive: SUPPORTED — QuantumCircuit.delay in Qiskit SDK; OpenQASM 3 'delay[Nus|Nns|Ndt] qubit' on hardware after transpilation. dt/ns/us/ms/s units all supported.
candidates.delay_caveats: Qiskit SDK does not yet parse 'duration'/'stretch' type declarations (as of Jul 2025); explicit ns/us values work. Stretch DD limited.

free_tier.name: Open Plan
free_tier.promo: After 20 min compute in any 12mo, opt-in for 180 min over next 12mo
free_tier.hardware: Heron r2 ibm_kingston included for Open Plan as of 2026-03-16 announcement

candidates.korean_access: OPEN — IBM has active Korea Quantum Computing partnership (KQC, Busan); System Two installation planned 2028. No US export restriction on Open Plan cloud access for SK developers. Qiskit 1.0 explicitly distributed to KR education channels.
candidates.onboarding_lead_time: minutes — IBM Cloud account (free) + Quantum Platform sign-in. No interview, no waitlist for Open Plan.
candidates.mvp_cost_rationale: 4-qubit GHZ + 5 delay points (0us, 25us, 50us, 100us, 200us) x 100 shots = 500 shots. Single circuit batch ~30-60s wall-clock on ibm_kingston. Comfortably inside 10 min/28d Open Plan budget.
primary_strengths: Heron r2 T1/T2 ~100-300us appropriate for tau_2 measurement window
primary_risks: Open Plan queue wait can be hours
Heron coherence times not as long as Quantinuum but adequate

candidates.hardware: System Model H1 (20q fully connected), System Model H2 (56q fully connected, upgraded from 32q)
candidates.delay_primitive: SUPPORTED via TKET/pytket-quantinuum native instruction set — H-series control stack is independent from IonQ's; idle/wait operations are first-class.
candidates.coherence: T2* > 1 second (best in class trapped-ion); T1 > 100 s (optical-pumping limited). Two-qubit gate ~100-200 us, 1Q gate 1-10 us.

free_tier.name: Azure Free Trial credits
free_tier.valid_for_quantinuum: PARTIAL — Azure free credits eligible for some providers; Quantinuum free credit availability not guaranteed; pay-as-you-go quoted per-HQC by Quantinuum sales (not published)

candidates.korean_access: OPEN via Azure global tenant; no published export restriction. Quantinuum is Cambridge/Boulder, no ITAR for cloud H1/H2 use.
candidates.onboarding_lead_time: 1-3 days — Azure subscription, workspace creation, provider enable. PAYG requires sales contact for HQC rate quote.
candidates.mvp_cost_rationale: GHZ4 ~ 6 1Q + 3 2Q + 4 SPAM, plus 5 delay points -> conservatively 15 1Q + 5 2Q + 5 SPAM per circuit. 5 circuits x 100 shots. HQC = 5 + 100*(15 + 50 + 25)/5000 = 5 + 1.8 = 6.8 HQC per circuit -> 34 HQC total. Quantinuum has not published USD/HQC for PAYG; Strangeworks-style estimates ~$1-10/HQC -> $34-$340 range. Sales contact required for exact quote.
primary_strengths: Best-in-class trapped-ion T2* >1s — same physical family as IonQ but with delay support
primary_risks: PAYG cost opaque (sales contact required, violates $0/no-vendor-contact constraint)
Subscription tiers $125k+/mo are not relevant for $50 MVP

candidates.platform: ionq.com Quantum Cloud (direct, bypassing AWS Braket)
candidates.delay_primitive: UNCONFIRMED at IR level — IonQ Direct API exposes lower-level pulse control than AWS Braket abstraction, but public docs do not enumerate a 'delay' instruction. The same physical hardware (Forte 1) failed delay validation via AWS Braket; whether the Direct API exposes additional capabilities is not publicly verified. Native gate set still GPI/GPI2/ZZ.
candidates.delay_evidence_status: INSUFFICIENT — would require account creation (violates constraint) or pre-sales technical inquiry

paid_tier.rates: publicly available only via Azure intermediary (see Azure pricing); IonQ Direct does not publish per-shot USD on public pricing page
paid_tier.Azure_reference_AQT_formula: AQT = m + 0.000220*(N1q*C) + 0.000975*(N2q*C); m = $97.50 (EM on) or $12.42 (EM off) for Aria; m = $168.20 (EM on) / $25.79 (EM off) for Forte 1

candidates.korean_access: OPEN in principle (commercial cloud) but IonQ has no published KR onboarding flow; sales-driven.
candidates.onboarding_lead_time: 1-4 weeks — application + sales discussion required; not self-serve.
candidates.mvp_cost_for_F_N12_1_v3_USD_estimate: 129-840 USD via Azure equivalent (5 circuits x $25.79 minimum on Forte 1 EM-off)
candidates.mvp_cost_rationale: 5 circuits each pays the Forte 1 minimum-per-program fee. Even with delay supported, cost dominated by per-program minimum.
primary_strengths: Same Forte 1 hardware as v2 (apples-to-apples comparison)
primary_risks: Delay support UNCONFIRMED (same hardware that failed in v2)

candidates.delay_primitive: SUPPORTED via OpenPulse / OpenQASM 3 pulse-level access. Delay can be applied to qubits in pulse sequences on Amazon Braket Rigetti devices.

free_tier.name: AWS Free Tier (Braket)
free_tier.amount: 1 hour simulator time/month + Welcome Bonus credits ($100-300 case-by-case)

paid_tier.cost_5_circuits_500_shots_USD: ~$1.71-$2.55 (depending on whether all delay points fit one task)

candidates.korean_access: OPEN — AWS Braket available in KR via us-east-1 access (same path as v2 N-12 used).
candidates.onboarding_lead_time: 0 — AWS account already exists per v2 (267673635495 anima-braket-cli IAM, [braket] profile, us-east-1).
candidates.mvp_cost_rationale: 5 delay points, each as separate task @ $0.30 task fee + 100 shots @ $0.000425 = $0.34/task * 5 = $1.71. Or 1 task with 500 shots = $0.51. Per-program minimum is small.
primary_strengths: Existing AWS account + IAM (zero new onboarding)
primary_risks: NOT a trapped-ion comparison — changes physical substrate vs Forte 1, breaking ion-vs-SC ratio test logic
Different decoherence physics (T1 ~30-100us SC vs ~seconds ion); ratio test would compare two SC devices, defeating original Orch-OR cross-substrate purpose unless reframed

rationale: 1. Delay primitive officially supported in OpenQASM 3 + Qiskit, evidence: IBM qasm-feature-table lists delay as supported across SDK and Runtime with ns/us/ms/dt units.
2. Cost = $0 USD for the entire MVP (10 min/28d Open Plan covers 5 circuits x 100 shots).
3. Onboarding = minutes (IBM Cloud account, no waitlist).
4. Korea-friendly: KQC partnership active, no export restriction on Open Plan.
5. Heron r2 T1/T2 (50-300us) sit in the right window to discriminate Penrose-Hameroff predicted ~25 us neuronal coherence threshold without being so long as to mask the signal.

6. Honest tradeoff: IBM is superconducting, not trapped-ion. Ratio test reframed as 'IBM SC delay-decoherence vs Penrose-Hameroff predicted neuronal tau' rather than 'ion vs SC'. Substrate-cross comparison shifts to IBM-Heron-vs-PRNewswire-SC-reference (within-family) plus IBM-Heron-vs-neuronal-Penrose (cross-family).
top_1_recommendation.reframing_note: Original F-N12-1 logic was ion-substrate (Forte 1) vs SC reference. With Forte 1 substrate-incapable and IonQ Direct unverified, pivoting to IBM SC means we now test SC-decoherence-vs-Penrose-prediction directly. Cross-substrate ion comparison becomes a follow-up (deferred to N-12 v4 with Quantinuum H1/H2 if needed).
